text: Ever wonder what happens in your brain when you daydream? It's called the default mode network, a web of regions that light up when your mind wanders. This network plays a role in self-reflection, creativity, and problem-solving. Studies suggest this network is active during mind-wandering, but research is still emerging. So, next time you drift off, know your brain is working hard in the background. Curious about more brain mysteries? Hit that like button and follow for more!
